briefdescription.para: Don't forget to add all the new fields to serialization.

detaileddescription.para: Whether this is a conical angle. The middle line between the rays is preserved, but the rays themselves can be rotated.

briefdescription.para: Whether we should draw a ray from the center point to better visualize the angle. Enable this if there isn't already a line object there.
The xf encodes the two rays: the origin is the angle point, (1,0,0) is the first ray, (0,1,0) is the second ray.

detaileddescription.para: clones this object only, without its children, making new object the owner of all copied resources
clones this object only, without its children, making new object to share resources with this object

briefdescription.para: Get the angle point in world coordinates.
Get the angle point in local coordinates.

detaileddescription.para: One of the two rays representing the angle, relative to the starting point. They can have length != 1 for visualization purposes, it's probably a good idea to take the smaller of the two lengths.

briefdescription.para: Set the angle point in the local coordinates.

detaileddescription.para: Set the two rays representing the angle in the local coordinates. The lengths are preserved.

briefdescription.para: Whether this is a conical angle. The middle line between the rays is preserved, but the rays themselves can be rotated.
Whether we should draw a ray from the center point to better visualize the angle. Enable this if there isn't already a line object there.
Computes the angle value, as if by acos(dot(...)) from the two normalized getWorldRay()s.
return several info lines that can better describe the object in the UI

detaileddescription.para:
Write parameters to given Json::Value,
if you override this method, please call Base::serializeFields_(root) in the beginning

Reads parameters from json value
if you override this method, please call Base::deserializeFields_(root) in the beginning
each renderable child of VisualObject should implement this method and assign renderObj_ inside

briefdescription.para: Emits worldXfChangedSignal, but derived classes can add additional behavior to it.
